scale.5: Consistently utilizes symptoms and ongoing patterns as clues to look more deeply at the underlying, related, or ongoing causes of problems and issues.
scale.3: Searches for and recognizes symptoms that indicate problems; accurately differentiates the problems from the symptoms.
scale.1: Focuses on surface symptoms, rather than the underlying source of problems.

PRS.indicator: Come up with solution hypotheses for problems

scale.5: Consistently generates multiple well-reasoned, innovative solution hypotheses for complex problems. Uses data and insights to validate ideas before implementation.
scale.3: Proposes reasonable solution hypotheses based on available information but may require refinement. Occasionally needs guidance to validate and improve solutions.
scale.1: Struggles to generate viable solution hypotheses. Offers vague or impractical ideas, often relying on others to drive problem-solving efforts.

PRS.indicator: Analyzes, incorporates and applies new information and concepts.

scale.5: Immediately recognizes when new information or concepts are relevant, understands the implications, and appropriately incorporates all new inputs into current thinking.
scale.3: Analyzes, incorporates, and applies new information and concepts.
scale.1: Acknowledges new pieces of information and concepts, but may be slow to do so, or may have trouble applying them effectively.

STG.indicator: Understand the short- and medium-term consequences of own actions

scale.5: Consistently anticipates and evaluates the impact of own actions on tasks, colleagues, and the organization, taking proactive steps to enhance positive outcomes and mitigate risks.
scale.3: Understands the consequences of own actions in most situations but may need occasional guidance to assess broader implications.
scale.1: Rarely considers the consequences of own actions, often leading to unforeseen issues or disruptions.

scale.5: Clearly understands and effectively navigates hierarchical structures, leveraging organizational relationships to improve collaboration and decision-making.
scale.3: Has a functional understanding of organizational hierarchy and relationships but may struggle to use them effectively in complex situations.
scale.1: Lacks awareness of hierarchical structures and organizational relationships, leading to ineffective communication and poor decision-making.

STG.indicator: Know the purpose of the organization and how the main processes work

scale.5: Demonstrates deep understanding of the organization’s purpose, mission, and processes, aligning work effectively with business goals and identifying opportunities for improvement.
scale.3: Has a general awareness of the organization’s purpose and main processes but may not always see how their role contributes to broader objectives.
scale.1: Lacks understanding of the organization’s purpose and processes, resulting in misalignment with company goals and inefficiencies in work.

INN.indicator: Approaches problems with curiosity and open-mindedness.

scale.5: Consistently approaches problems with strong curiosity and open-mindedness.
scale.3: Demonstrates openness and curiosity when approaching problems.
scale.1: Displays little curiosity or open-mindedness when presented with problems to resolve.

INN.indicator: Generates innovative ideas and solutions to problems.

scale.5: Generates highly innovative ideas, solutions, and opportunities that challenge status quo thinking and assumptions.
scale.3: Generates innovative ideas, solutions, and new perspectives in reaction to issues; is open to challenging current processes and procedures.
scale.1: Generates status-quo ideas and solutions, and/or resists others' efforts to challenge current processes and procedures.

INN.indicator: Quickly adapt to new ways of doing things

scale.5: Embraces change proactively, quickly adjusting to new methods with minimal disruption. Encourages others to adapt and finds innovative ways to optimize transitions.
scale.3: Adapts to new methods with some hesitation but eventually aligns with changes. Requires some support or guidance to fully implement new ways of working.
scale.1: Resists change, struggles to adapt to new methods, and requires excessive support. Often reverts to old ways, slowing down team progress.

DRV.indicator: Maintain a consistent, high level of productivity

scale.5: Consistently exceeds productivity expectations, delivering high-quality work efficiently. Effectively manages workload even under pressure.
scale.3: Meets productivity expectations, completing tasks in a timely and competent manner. Occasionally struggles with workload balance.
scale.1: Frequently falls short of productivity expectations, requiring reminders or interventions to complete tasks on time. Struggles to maintain efficiency.

DRV.indicator: Organize own work and manage time wisely

scale.5: Demonstrates exceptional time management and organizational skills, prioritizing tasks effectively and completing work ahead of deadlines.
scale.3: Organizes work adequately and manages time well, though occasional prioritization issues may arise. Meets most deadlines without significant intervention.
scale.1: Struggles to organize work, often missing deadlines or requiring constant supervision. Poor prioritization leads to inefficiencies.

DRV.indicator: Respond promptly to requests also managing conflicting priorities

scale.5: Handles multiple requests efficiently, responding promptly while balancing conflicting priorities with minimal disruption. Proactively communicates if adjustments are needed.
scale.3: Responds to requests in a timely manner but may struggle to balance multiple priorities effectively at times. Requires occasional guidance to manage workload.
scale.1: Frequently delays responses or fails to manage conflicting priorities, leading to missed deadlines and frustration for stakeholders.

scale.5: Proactively seeks to understand customer needs and interests, anticipating and addressing concerns effectively. Provides tailored solutions that exceed expectations.
scale.3: Understands customer needs and interests at a basic level, responding appropriately to requests but may not always anticipate needs.
scale.1: Demonstrates little effort to understand customer needs, often providing generic or ineffective responses. May cause frustration due to lack of attention to detail.

scale.5: Consistently follows through on commitments to customers, ensuring reliability and trust. Proactively communicates any necessary adjustments.
scale.3: Generally keeps commitments to customers but may occasionally require reminders or extensions. Communicates when delays occur.
scale.1: Frequently fails to meet commitments, leading to customer dissatisfaction. Does not communicate effectively when unable to fulfill obligations.

CSF.indicator: Establish effective working relationships with internal and external customers

scale.5: Builds and maintains strong, professional relationships with internal and external customers. Communicates effectively, fostering collaboration and trust.
scale.3: Maintains satisfactory relationships with customers, though interactions may sometimes lack consistency or engagement.
scale.1: Struggles to establish or maintain effective relationships, often leading to misunderstandings or conflicts with customers.

scale.5: Provides detailed, precise, and engaging descriptions of products and services, ensuring customers fully understand their value and benefits. Tailors explanations to different audiences.
scale.3: Describes products and services correctly but may lack depth or adaptation to different customer needs. Occasionally needs support for complex details.
scale.1: Provides vague, incorrect, or misleading descriptions of products and services, leading to confusion or dissatisfaction. Lacks product knowledge.

SLF.indicator: Link own arguments to customer needs and priorities

scale.5: Skillfully aligns arguments with customer needs, demonstrating deep understanding and persuasive communication. Uses data and insights to strengthen connections.
scale.3: Makes an effort to connect arguments with customer needs but may not always be compelling or fully aligned. Can improve customization of messaging.
scale.1: Fails to link arguments to customer priorities, providing generic or irrelevant points. Struggles to engage or persuade customers effectively.

SLF.indicator: Negotiate to reach solutions that meet the primary interests of customers

scale.5: Effectively negotiates win-win solutions, balancing customer needs with business objectives. Demonstrates patience, active listening, and creative problem-solving.
scale.3: Engages in negotiation and reaches acceptable solutions, though may require additional support or refinement in handling complex situations.
scale.1: Struggles with negotiation, often failing to reach mutually beneficial solutions. May come across as inflexible or unable to address customer concerns.

OPX.indicator: Perform tasks correctly and on time

scale.5: Consistently completes tasks with high accuracy and ahead of deadlines. Manages workload efficiently, even under pressure.
scale.3: Completes tasks correctly and on time most of the time but may require occasional follow-ups or reminders.
scale.1: Frequently misses deadlines or submits work with errors. Requires constant supervision to complete tasks.

OPX.indicator: Check own work to ensure accuracy and quality

scale.5: Regularly reviews work thoroughly, identifying and correcting errors before submission. Ensures high-quality results with minimal oversight.
scale.3: Occasionally checks work for accuracy and quality but may overlook minor errors. Requires some guidance to improve attention to detail.
scale.1: Rarely reviews work, leading to frequent mistakes and quality issues. Requires significant intervention to ensure accuracy.

OPX.indicator: Contribute to the achievement of own team goals

scale.5: Actively collaborates with team members, takes initiative, and contributes innovative ideas to achieve team goals. Supports others when needed.
scale.3: Participates in team efforts and completes assigned responsibilities but may not always go beyond basic expectations.
scale.1: Contributes minimally to team goals, often focusing only on individual tasks without considering team success. May cause delays or conflicts.

COM.indicator: Get the point across clearly and concisely

scale.5: Communicates messages with clarity and precision, ensuring understanding with minimal repetition. Uses well-structured and concise language suited to the audience.
scale.3: Expresses thoughts clearly but may sometimes provide unnecessary details or require clarification. Generally effective but could improve conciseness.
scale.1: Struggles to communicate clearly, often using vague or confusing language. Requires frequent clarification, leading to misunderstandings.

scale.5: Engages the audience effectively through confident delivery, relevant examples, and dynamic tone. Adapts to audience reactions to maintain interest.
scale.3: Keeps listeners engaged but may rely on standard delivery techniques. Occasionally struggles to hold attention in longer discussions.
scale.1: Fails to maintain audience interest, often speaking in a monotone or lacking structure. Listeners become disengaged or distracted.

scale.5: Contributes confidently and articulately in group settings, ensuring ideas are well-received. Encourages dialogue and integrates diverse viewpoints.
scale.3: Participates effectively in group discussions but may occasionally struggle with clarity, confidence, or engagement.
scale.1: Has difficulty expressing ideas in group settings, either remaining silent or dominating conversations without consideration for others.

scale.5: Collaborates seamlessly with colleagues, fostering a positive and productive work environment. Listens actively and values input from others.
scale.3: Works well with colleagues but may encounter occasional difficulties in teamwork. Open to feedback and willing to contribute.
scale.1: Struggles to collaborate, often working in isolation or causing conflicts within the team. Resists feedback.

scale.5: Proactively offers assistance to colleagues, even before being asked. Provides support with a positive attitude and fosters a culture of teamwork.
scale.3: Helps colleagues when requested but may not always take the initiative to offer support.
scale.1: Avoids helping colleagues, showing reluctance or lack of concern for team challenges.

COL.indicator: Demonstrate commitment and dedication to team goals

scale.5: Consistently prioritizes team goals, actively contributing to shared success. Shows enthusiasm and persistence in overcoming obstacles.
scale.3: Supports team goals and contributes to objectives but may not take a proactive role in ensuring success.
scale.1: Shows little commitment to team goals, focusing only on personal tasks or disengaging from team efforts.

scale.5: Effectively persuades and influences others through logical reasoning, empathy, and strong communication. Gains enthusiastic support for ideas.
scale.3: Gains support from others but may struggle in challenging situations. Uses basic persuasion techniques but could improve influence.
scale.1: Fails to gain support due to unclear communication, lack of credibility, or inability to address concerns. Often faces resistance.

INF.indicator: Ensure that own positions address others' needs and priorities.

scale.5: Consistently tailors arguments and decisions to align with others' needs and priorities, fostering mutual understanding and cooperation.
scale.3: Attempts to consider others’ needs when presenting positions but may not always address them effectively.
scale.1: Rarely considers others’ needs, presenting positions in a one-sided manner that leads to resistance or disengagement.

INF.indicator: Provide sound rationale to support opinions and proposals

scale.5: Backs up opinions and proposals with strong, well-researched arguments, data, and logical reasoning, making a compelling case.
scale.3: Provides some rationale for opinions but may lack depth or supporting evidence. Can explain reasoning but not always persuasively.
scale.1: Struggles to justify opinions or proposals with logic or evidence, often leading to weak arguments that fail to convince others.

NET.indicator: Easily connect with people at all organizational levels

scale.5: Builds rapport effortlessly with individuals at all levels, adapting communication style appropriately and fostering trust.
scale.3: Connects well with most people but may struggle with higher-level stakeholders or unfamiliar audiences.
scale.1: Has difficulty engaging with others, leading to ineffective interactions or strained relationships.

NET.indicator: Listen to others carefully and without interrupting

scale.5: Actively listens, demonstrating full attention and patience. Responds thoughtfully and respectfully, making others feel valued.
scale.3: Listens attentively but may occasionally interrupt or get distracted. Usually responds appropriately.
scale.1: Frequently interrupts, appears disengaged, or fails to acknowledge others’ input, leading to frustration.

NET.indicator: Establish and maintain effective working relationships with people from own organizational unit

scale.5: Consistently fosters strong, collaborative relationships with colleagues, contributing to a supportive and productive work environment.
scale.3: Maintains functional working relationships with team members but may not proactively strengthen them.
scale.1: Struggles to build or sustain working relationships, leading to misunderstandings, conflicts, or isolation.

RES.indicator: Demonstrate flexibility and adaptability when priorities change

scale.5: Quickly adjusts to shifting priorities with a positive attitude, maintaining productivity and helping others adapt. Seeks solutions to challenges proactively.
scale.3: Adapts to changing priorities with some initial hesitation but eventually aligns with new directions. May require occasional support.
scale.1: Resists change, struggles to adjust, and often slows down team progress when priorities shift. Needs constant guidance to adapt.

scale.5: Maintains composure and focus even in high-pressure situations. Thinks clearly, makes rational decisions, and reassures others.
scale.3: Handles pressure adequately but may show occasional signs of stress. Manages tasks but could improve emotional control.
scale.1: Easily overwhelmed under pressure, leading to panic, errors, or negative interactions with others.

scale.5: Takes full ownership of mistakes, proactively addressing them and implementing corrective actions. Uses mistakes as learning opportunities.
scale.3: Admits mistakes when confronted and works on improvements but may not always take initiative in addressing them.
scale.1: Avoids or denies mistakes, shifts blame, or makes excuses, leading to repeated errors and lost trust.

LRN.indicator: Accept feedback openly, without getting defensive

scale.5: Seeks out feedback proactively, listens attentively, and applies constructive criticism to improve performance.
scale.3: Accepts feedback with some hesitation but makes an effort to improve. Occasionally needs reminders to remain receptive.
scale.1: Reacts defensively or dismisses feedback, refusing to acknowledge areas for improvement.

LRN.indicator: Seeks opportunities to acquire new knowledge and skills.

scale.5: Actively pursues learning opportunities, staying ahead of trends and continuously expanding knowledge and skills.
scale.3: Engages in learning when required but does not consistently seek out new opportunities independently.
scale.1: Shows little interest in learning, relying only on existing knowledge and avoiding opportunities for growth.

scale.5: Demonstrates strong curiosity and enthusiasm for learning, taking initiative to explore new topics and apply knowledge.
scale.3: Willing to learn when prompted but does not always take initiative. Shows interest in learning but may need encouragement.
scale.1: Lacks curiosity and motivation to learn new things. Avoids challenges and resists new information.

ITG.indicator: Know and respect the organization's values and standards of conduct

scale.5: Fully understands, upholds, and promotes the organization’s values and conduct standards. Acts as a role model and encourages others to follow them.
scale.3: Follows organizational values and standards but may need occasional reminders. Does not actively promote them.
scale.1: Disregards or violates organizational values and conduct standards, creating ethical or reputational risks.

scale.5: Consistently acts with integrity, transparency, and sincerity. Earns trust by being truthful and fair in all interactions.
scale.3: Generally honest and sincere but may occasionally withhold information or be unclear in difficult situations.
scale.1: Engages in dishonest or insincere behavior, misleading others or failing to maintain trust.

scale.5: Always follows through on commitments, ensuring reliability and accountability. Proactively communicates if adjustments are needed.
scale.3: Keeps most commitments but may occasionally fail to meet expectations or require reminders.
scale.1: Frequently breaks commitments, causing delays, mistrust, or frustration among colleagues and stakeholders.

INC.indicator: Build constructive relationships with people different of self

scale.5: Actively seeks to understand and collaborate with diverse individuals. Values different perspectives and fosters an inclusive environment.
scale.3: Works well with different people but may not actively seek out diverse perspectives or relationships.
scale.1: Struggles to connect with people different from self, showing little interest in understanding or engaging with them.

INC.indicator: Avoid using cultural stereotypes to make judgments about people.

scale.5: Treats individuals with respect and fairness, avoiding assumptions based on stereotypes. Actively challenges bias in oneself and others.
scale.3: Generally avoids stereotypes but may occasionally make assumptions unconsciously. Open to learning and improving.
scale.1: Frequently relies on cultural stereotypes to judge others, leading to biased decisions and strained relationships.

scale.5: Demonstrates strong cultural awareness, adapting communication and behavior to work effectively across cultures. Shows deep respect for different traditions and norms.
scale.3: Acknowledges cultural differences and makes an effort to adapt, though may need guidance in unfamiliar situations.
scale.1: Struggles to recognize or adapt to cultural differences, leading to misunderstandings or ineffective interactions.
